refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR bundles three distinct obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate functional concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies implementation choice) and focuses on the core problem: making worksite geometry and features available in a structured, queryable form. Splitting into atomic sub-FRs allows independent DP selection and IM packaging.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Confirm that a worksite model exists in a defined schema (e.g., point cloud, mesh, or feature database) and can be queried by downstream planning and execution functions to retrieve spatial attributes (position, geometry, classification) for an agreed set of site features.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface geometry data from the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and classify static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent work-zone boundaries and restricted areas in the spatial model.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Persist and provide access to the worksite spatial model for planning and execution functions.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data through multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured geometric model (point clouds, meshes, or voxel grids) with semantic labels and metadata; expose via query interface for downstream planning/execution consumers.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign each zone a feature inventory (obstacles, hazards, access points) via manual annotation or semi-automated detection; represent as a graph of zone nodes with edge constraints; persist in a queryable schema.

DP3: Capture worksite features as a rule-based symbolic representation (e.g., logic rules or ontology) that encodes spatial relationships, safety constraints, and operational policies; derive a queryable model on demand by evaluating rules against sensor observations.

DP4: Establish a real-time streaming pipeline that continuously ingests sensor data, applies feature extraction and filtering, and maintains an incrementally updated spatial model; expose model state via subscription/polling interface for planning/execution.

DP5: Delegate feature capture to a hybrid approach: use pre-defined templates (e.g., site layout blueprints, CAD models) as a baseline; augment with live sensor observations to detect deviations, dynamic hazards, and transient features; merge into a composite model.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Hybrid baseline-augmented model. Establish a composite spatial representation by anchoring to pre-defined worksite templates (blueprints, CAD, or prior surveys) as a static baseline; continuously augment with live multi-modal sensor observations to detect and integrate dynamic features (moving obstacles, temporary hazards, personnel), deviations from baseline, and real-time operational state. Expose the merged model via a structured query interface (spatial queries, feature filters, constraint checks) that supports both planning and execution consumers. Maintain traceability between baseline and observed features to support safety auditing and policy compliance.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between sensor modality selection and feature extraction logic: mitigated by defining a sensor-agnostic feature schema and abstraction layer.
Dependency on baseline template availability and accuracy: mitigated by graceful degradation (operate with live-only model if baseline unavailable) and versioning.
Coupling between model update frequency and planning/execution cycle time: mitigated by decoupling via asynchronous query interface and explicit freshness metadata.
Policy/safety constraint representation tightly bound to model schema: mitigated by externalizing constraints as a separate, versioned policy layer queryable against the model.
coupling_check.notes: DP5 achieves loose coupling by separating concerns: baseline (static, pre-computed), live observations (dynamic, sensor-driven), and query interface (consumer-agnostic). The hybrid approach avoids over-dependence on real-time sensing alone (DP1 risk) or manual annotation brittleness (DP2 risk), and defers rule evaluation overhead (DP3) while supporting incremental updates (DP4 benefit).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) are available and reasonably accurate at mission start. Risk: If baseline is stale or unavailable, model degrades to live-only; mitigation: define fallback mode and baseline versioning strategy.

Assumption: Multi-modal sensor suite (vision, range, inertial) is available and calibrated. Risk: Sensor failures or miscalibration corrupt feature extraction; mitigation: implement sensor health monitoring and feature confidence scoring.

Assumption: Feature extraction and fusion algorithms can reliably distinguish dynamic hazards from static baseline. Risk: False positives (noise) or false negatives (missed hazards) compromise safety; mitigation: require human-in-the-loop validation for safety-critical features and define confidence thresholds.

Assumption: Query interface latency and model update frequency meet planning/execution cycle time requirements. Risk: Stale model data leads to unsafe or inefficient decisions; mitigation: define SLA for model freshness and implement priority-based update scheduling.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are formalized and queryable. Risk: Policies are ambiguous or incomplete; mitigation: require explicit policy schema and validation against model before execution.

Assumption: Baseline and live observations can be reliably registered in a common coordinate frame. Risk: Registration drift or errors propagate through model; mitigation: implement continuous re-registration and drift detection.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 by explicitly defining: (1) the baseline template schema (geometry, feature types, metadata); (2) the live observation schema and confidence/freshness metadata; (3) the merge/reconciliation logic (conflict resolution, priority rules); (4) the query interface contract (spatial predicates, feature filters, constraint checks); (5) the policy layer schema and binding to model features; (6) the fallback/degradation strategy if baseline or sensors fail. Introduce an IM layer to clarify ownership: baseline management (IM-Baseline), live sensor fusion (IM-Fusion), model storage (IM-Store), query service (IM-Query), policy engine (IM-Policy). This decomposition supports independent development, testing, and evolution of each module while maintaining clear interfaces.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized spatial representation engine that maintains hybrid baseline-augmented models, manages sensor data fusion, provides structured query interfaces for planning and execution, and maintains feature traceability for compliance auditing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingest live multi-modal sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, thermal, radar) from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems to continuously augment baseline model with dynamic features and operational state.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Expose structured query interface (spatial queries, feature filters, constraint checks) to planning and execution consumers for mission planning and real-time operational decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emit safety-relevant deviations from baseline, detected hazards, and constraint violations; support traceability logs for safety auditing and policy compliance verification.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Receive baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) and synchronize hybrid model state across distributed planning/execution nodes; support remote query and audit access.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit. NGHE is the natural locus for spatial representation, sensor fusion, and query logic. Baseline management, augmentation logic, and traceability are core to a ground-truth environment model. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry ingestion (Vehicle/Teleoperation) and query consumption (Tasking/Safety) cross boundaries via well-defined interfaces.

Poor fit. Vehicle is a platform/agent, not a model repository. Embedding baseline management and query logic here would scatter spatial reasoning across multiple vehicle instances, violate single-source-of-truth principle, and create tight coupling between vehicle control and environment representation. High diffusion to Teleoperation, Safety Services, and planning layers.

Poor fit. Teleoperation is an operator interface/control channel, not a model engine. Placing baseline-augmented model logic here would conflate operator interaction with environment representation, create redundancy if multiple vehicles/operators exist, and diffuse spatial reasoning across control and perception domains.

Moderate-to-poor fit. Safety Services consumes the hybrid model (constraint checks, hazard detection, compliance auditing) but should not own the model itself. Placing baseline management and sensor fusion here would couple safety logic to representation maintenance, violate separation of concerns, and create diffusion to planning and execution layers that also need the model.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The hybrid baseline-augmented model is a shared, authoritative spatial representation that serves multiple consumers (planning, execution, safety, teleoperation). It is most cohesively owned by NGHE Solution as the central environment model engine. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are consumers that query and react to this model via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking/SafetyAlert out). This separation preserves single-source-of-truth, enables multi-agent/multi-operator scenarios, and isolates representation logic from control and safety policy logic.
containment_risks: Network latency: If baseline templates or augmented model updates are delayed over Network Link, planning and execution may operate on stale representations. Mitigation: implement local caching and conflict resolution in NGHE Solution.

Sensor fusion complexity: Integrating multi-modal observations (LiDAR, camera, thermal, radar) with baseline templates requires robust data association and outlier handling. If fusion logic is incomplete, augmented features may be unreliable. Mitigation: validate fusion algorithms and maintain confidence/uncertainty metadata in the model.

Traceability overhead: Maintaining full provenance (baseline vs. observed, sensor source, timestamp, confidence) for every feature increases storage and query latency. Mitigation: implement efficient indexing and archival policies.

Query interface design: If the structured query interface (spatial queries, feature filters, constraint checks) is too rigid or incomplete, consumers may bypass it or request ad-hoc extensions, leading to diffusion. Mitigation: design query interface iteratively with planning, execution, and safety stakeholders.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The FR ('Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model') is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria: (1) worksite model/map generation and availability to planning/execution for agreed site features, and (2) alignment with worksite safety plan and operational policies. The IM boundary (NGHE Solution) is a concrete, testable module with measurable interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking/SafetyAlert out, Network Link sync). Verification can proceed via: model completeness audits against baseline templates, feature detection accuracy metrics, query latency/correctness validation, and traceability log inspection for compliance auditing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Excellent. The allocation from FR to DP to IM is clear and traceable. FR defines the problem (spatial feature capture and representation). DP5 specifies the solution approach (hybrid baseline-augmented model with sensor fusion, query interface, and traceability). NGHE Solution is the unambiguous owner with a 0.92 containment score and well-defined external interfaces. The single-source-of-truth principle is preserved: Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are explicitly positioned as consumers, not owners, eliminating ambiguity about responsibility boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 (Hybrid baseline-augmented model) directly and sufficiently addresses FR (Capture and represent worksite spatial features). The DP specifies: (1) baseline anchoring (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) for static reference, (2) continuous augmentation with live multi-modal sensor observations for dynamic features, (3) structured query interface for planning/execution consumers, and (4) traceability for safety auditing. This covers the full scope of the FR. The DP is implementable and testable against acceptance criteria (model availability, feature agreement, policy compliance). No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Sensor fusion logic diffusion: Multi-modal sensor ingestion (LiDAR, camera, thermal, radar) is specified as an inbound Telemetry interface, but the actual fusion algorithms (data association, outlier rejection, confidence estimation) must remain fully contained within NGHE Solution. Risk: if fusion logic is partially implemented in Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems (e.g., pre-filtering, feature extraction), the authoritative model becomes inconsistent. Mitigation: enforce strict API contract on Telemetry interface—raw or minimally-processed sensor streams only; all fusion logic centralized in NGHE.

Baseline template management diffusion: Network Link interface receives baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys), but the authority for baseline versioning, conflict resolution, and synchronization across distributed nodes must remain in NGHE Solution. Risk: if planning or execution nodes cache and modify baselines locally, traceability and single-source-of-truth are compromised. Mitigation: implement read-only baseline distribution; all updates flow through NGHE with version control and audit logging.

Query interface scope creep: Tasking interface exposes 'spatial queries, feature filters, constraint checks' but the boundary between query consumption and query logic ownership is implicit. Risk: if planning or safety consumers implement custom spatial reasoning (e.g., collision detection, path feasibility checks) locally instead of via the query interface, the model becomes diffused. Mitigation: design query interface comprehensively with stakeholders upfront; document which spatial reasoning operations are NGHE-owned vs. consumer-owned.

Traceability provenance diffusion: SafetyAlert interface emits 'deviations from baseline, detected hazards, constraint violations' with traceability logs, but the definition of what constitutes a 'deviation' or 'hazard' may be policy-dependent. Risk: if Safety Services or planning layers define their own deviation detection logic instead of querying NGHE, traceability becomes fragmented. Mitigation: centralize deviation/hazard detection rules in NGHE; expose them via query interface for transparency and auditability.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution exhibits excellent containment (0.92 score) with minimal diffusion (0.08). The module boundary is well-defined: NGHE owns the hybrid baseline-augmented model, sensor fusion, query logic, and traceability. Vehicle (0.25 containment, 0.75 diffusion), Teleoperation (0.3 containment, 0.7 diffusion), and Safety Services (0.45 containment, 0.55 diffusion) are correctly positioned as consumers. The four external interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link inout) are well-articulated and minimize cross-boundary responsibility. Key strength: single-source-of-truth principle is preserved, enabling multi-agent and multi-operator scenarios without model duplication. Key risk: interface contracts must be strictly enforced to prevent fusion logic, baseline management, and spatial reasoning from leaking into consumer modules. Recommend detailed interface specifications (data schemas, API contracts, error handling) to lock down the DP<->IM bridge.

recommended_revisions: Formalize Telemetry interface contract: specify raw/minimally-processed sensor data formats (LiDAR point clouds, camera frames, thermal images, radar detections) and explicitly prohibit pre-fusion or feature extraction in Vehicle/Teleoperation. All fusion logic must be NGHE-owned.

Define baseline template versioning and synchronization protocol: specify how blueprints, CAD, and prior surveys are ingested, versioned, and distributed via Network Link. Establish read-only access for consumers; all baseline updates must flow through NGHE with audit logging.

Develop comprehensive Tasking query interface specification: enumerate supported spatial queries (e.g., 'find all obstacles within radius R', 'check constraint C at location L', 'retrieve feature history for audit'), feature filters (by type, confidence, timestamp, source), and constraint checks (collision, safety zone, policy compliance). Document which spatial reasoning operations are NGHE-owned vs. consumer-owned to prevent diffusion.

Specify SafetyAlert and traceability schema: define the structure of deviation/hazard alerts (baseline deviation, detected hazard, constraint violation), including provenance metadata (baseline feature ID, observed feature ID, sensor source, timestamp, confidence, policy rule triggered). Ensure traceability logs are immutable and queryable for compliance auditing.

Implement network latency and caching strategy: document how NGHE Solution handles delayed baseline updates or augmented model synchronization across distributed nodes. Specify local caching policies, conflict resolution rules, and staleness thresholds to maintain consistency.

Validate sensor fusion robustness: establish acceptance criteria for multi-modal fusion (data association accuracy, outlier rejection rate, confidence estimation calibration). Require validation against ground truth (e.g., manual surveys, high-confidence sensor subsets) before deployment.

Design query interface iteratively with stakeholders: conduct design reviews with planning, execution, and safety teams to ensure the Tasking interface is complete, intuitive, and prevents ad-hoc extensions that could diffuse spatial reasoning logic.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence. Verification feasibility is high, allocation clarity is excellent, and containment is strong (0.92). However, the gate results expose four critical diffusion risks that require architectural enforcement at the next decomposition level: (1) sensor fusion logic must remain centralized in NGHE, (2) baseline template management must enforce read-only distribution, (3) query interface scope must be explicitly bounded to prevent spatial reasoning diffusion, and (4) traceability provenance must be centralized. These risks are best mitigated by deriving next-level sub-FRs that explicitly address interface contracts, fusion logic ownership, baseline synchronization, and traceability enforcement. Stopping here would leave these risks unresolved in the design; zigzagging down allows us to decompose the DP into sub-level FRs that lock down the DP<->IM bridge and prevent diffusion during detailed design and implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR5.1: Ingest and fuse multi-modal sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, thermal, radar) into a unified, confidence-weighted spatial representation without pre-processing or feature extraction in upstream Vehicle/Teleoperation modules.

FR5.2: Manage baseline spatial templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) with versioning, read-only distribution, and centralized update authority to preserve single-source-of-truth across distributed nodes.

FR5.3: Provide a comprehensive, bounded spatial query interface (feature retrieval, constraint checking, collision detection, policy compliance validation) that prevents consumers from implementing ad-hoc spatial reasoning logic.

FR5.4: Generate and maintain immutable, queryable traceability logs that record the provenance of all spatial features (baseline vs. augmented, sensor sources, confidence metrics, policy rules triggered) for safety auditing and compliance verification.

FR5.5: Synchronize the hybrid baseline-augmented model across distributed planning, execution, and safety nodes with defined latency bounds, caching policies, and conflict resolution rules to maintain consistency in multi-agent scenarios.

revise.why: The current DP5 (Hybrid baseline-augmented model with sensor fusion, query interface, and traceability) is logically sound but lacks explicit architectural constraints to prevent diffusion. Recommend revising DP5 to include: (1) strict Telemetry interface contract specifying raw/minimally-processed sensor data only, (2) centralized multi-modal fusion algorithm ownership in NGHE, (3) read-only baseline distribution protocol with version control, (4) comprehensive Tasking query interface specification with enumerated operations and ownership boundaries, (5) immutable traceability schema with provenance metadata, and (6) network synchronization strategy with latency/caching/conflict resolution rules. These revisions transform DP5 from a conceptual solution into an architecturally enforceable design that prevents the identified diffusion risks.

assumptions: NGHE Solution is the single authoritative owner of the hybrid baseline-augmented spatial model and all fusion logic.
Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems provide raw or minimally-processed sensor streams (no pre-fusion or feature extraction).
Planning, execution, and safety consumers access spatial information exclusively through the Tasking query interface, not by caching or modifying local copies of the model.
Baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) are distributed read-only via Network Link; all updates flow through NGHE with audit logging.
Traceability logs are immutable and queryable for compliance auditing; deviation/hazard detection rules are centralized in NGHE.
Network latency and distributed caching are managed with defined staleness thresholds and conflict resolution rules to maintain consistency.
Safety criticality is high; all spatial features and traceability records must be verifiable and auditable for regulatory compliance.
open_risks: Sensor fusion logic diffusion: If Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems implement pre-filtering, feature extraction, or partial fusion, the authoritative model becomes inconsistent. Mitigation: enforce strict Telemetry interface contract and conduct code reviews to verify raw data ingestion.

Baseline template management diffusion: If planning or execution nodes cache and modify baselines locally, single-source-of-truth is compromised. Mitigation: implement read-only baseline distribution with version control and audit logging; validate via traceability log inspection.

Query interface scope creep: If planning or safety consumers implement custom spatial reasoning (collision detection, path feasibility checks) locally, the model becomes diffused. Mitigation: design query interface comprehensively upfront; document ownership boundaries; conduct design reviews with stakeholders.

Traceability provenance diffusion: If Safety Services or planning layers define their own deviation/hazard detection logic, traceability becomes fragmented. Mitigation: centralize deviation/hazard detection rules in NGHE; expose via query interface for transparency.

Network synchronization consistency: Distributed nodes may experience stale or conflicting baseline/augmented model states if latency, caching, and conflict resolution are not well-defined. Mitigation: specify synchronization protocol with latency bounds, caching policies, and conflict resolution rules; validate via integration testing.

Sensor fusion robustness: Multi-modal fusion (LiDAR, camera, thermal, radar) may fail under adverse conditions (occlusion, sensor degradation, data association ambiguity). Mitigation: establish acceptance criteria for fusion accuracy; validate against ground truth before deployment.
evidence_needed: Detailed Telemetry interface specification: data schemas for raw sensor streams (LiDAR point clouds, camera frames, thermal images, radar detections), sampling rates, latency bounds, and explicit prohibition on pre-fusion/feature extraction.

Baseline template versioning and synchronization protocol: specification of how blueprints, CAD, and prior surveys are ingested, versioned, distributed, and updated; read-only access enforcement; audit logging schema.

Comprehensive Tasking query interface specification: enumerated spatial queries (feature retrieval, constraint checking, collision detection, policy compliance validation), feature filters (by type, confidence, timestamp, source), and ownership boundaries (NGHE-owned vs. consumer-owned spatial reasoning).

Traceability schema and immutability enforcement: structure of deviation/hazard alerts (baseline deviation, detected hazard, constraint violation) with provenance metadata (baseline feature ID, observed feature ID, sensor source, timestamp, confidence, policy rule triggered); immutability guarantees and queryability for compliance auditing.

Network synchronization strategy: latency bounds, caching policies, conflict resolution rules, and staleness thresholds for distributed baseline/augmented model synchronization; validation via integration testing across planning, execution, and safety nodes.

Sensor fusion validation results: multi-modal fusion accuracy metrics (data association accuracy, outlier rejection rate, confidence estimation calibration) validated against ground truth (manual surveys, high-confidence sensor subsets) under representative worksite conditions.
Design review records: stakeholder feedback from planning, execution, and safety teams on query interface completeness, ownership boundaries, and prevention of ad-hoc spatial reasoning diffusion.
